question: Which suspension component features accordion-style folds or ridges in the undercarriage shots? A. Control arm B. Tie rod end C. CV axle boot D. Brake caliper
What number is embossed on the rubber housing of the new ball joint?
What color is the stylized text watermark visible in the bottom-left corner of the frames? A. Silver B. White C. Brown D. Black
What entities are present and what are they doing?
canonical_answer: Dirty hands hold a new ball joint with a clean black boot next to an old rusty ball joint with a torn boot. A rusty metal control arm sits below a black rubber CV axle boot on the vehicle undercarriage. Surface rust covers the suspension assembly including the brake caliper and tie rod end. A white ChrisFix watermark is in the bottom left corner.

question: How many ball joints are visible in the dirty hands?

Describe the visual progression of the video, noting the specific components shown in the hands versus those visible on the vehicle undercarriage.
canonical_answer: At 0s, dirty hands hold a new ball joint next to an old rusty one; at 4s, the number 300 is visible on the new joint's rubber housing; at 6s, the view shifts to the vehicle undercarriage showing a rusty control arm; at 10s, the camera focuses on the suspension assembly including the tie rod; at 16s, the brake caliper assembly is clearly visible next to the CV axle boot.
